commit.message:
fix(auth): 429 rate limit backoff + admin_settings RLS simplification

1. Supabase client now uses custom fetch with automatic retry + exponential
   backoff for 429 responses. Prevents the auth death spiral where rate-limited
   token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT → clears session → retry → 429 again.

2. Simplified admin_settings RLS from has_role() dependency to simple
   user_id = auth.uid(). The has_role() check created a circular dependency
   with user_roles RLS, causing 42501 on INSERT for super_admin users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: fix admin_settings RLS INSERT policy (42501)

Split single FOR ALL policy into separate SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
policies. The combined policy was causing 42501 insufficient_privilege
on INSERT for super_admin users. Migration deployed via API.

flag so listeners are only registered once.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(driver): return None when invocation_directory equals target_root (#2709)

## Summary

- When `strip_prefix` succeeds but `invocation_directory` equals
`target_root`, it yields an empty path (`""`). Previously this returned
`Some("")`, causing incorrect path prefix handling downstream.
- Fix returns `None` in the empty-path case to correctly indicate no
subdirectory offset.
- Fix `parent_with_file_issues` to emit issues when `path_prefix` is
`None` (it was previously gated on `path_prefix.is_some()`, which
silently dropped issues like knip's `package.json` findings when running
from the root).
- Updates the Trivy README to reflect that Trivy is not automatically
enabled by `qlty init`.

## Motivation

Trivy plugin tests on Windows were failing due to this bug:
https://github.com/qltysh/qlty/actions/runs/22582724416/job/65419040073

The Trivy drivers use `runs_from = root_or_parent_with_any_config`,
which falls back to running from `target_root` when no config file is
found. This makes `invocation_directory == target_root`, causing
`strip_prefix` to yield an empty path. The bogus `Some("")` prefix was
then prepended to issue paths during parsing.

On macOS/Linux this accidentally worked because a subsequent
`strip_prefix('/')` call cleaned up the malformed paths. On Windows, the
different path separator semantics meant the mangled paths didn't match
workspace entries, causing the snapshot tests to fail.

Fixing `get_path_prefix` then exposed a second bug:
`parent_with_file_issues` was gated on `path_prefix.is_some()` as a
proxy for "running from a subdirectory." With `path_prefix` now
correctly `None` when running from the root, knip's `package.json`
issues were silently dropped. Fixed by using the target path directly
when there is no prefix.

## Test plan

- [x] `test_get_path_prefix_equal_dirs_returns_none` — covers the
primary bug fix (invocation dir == target root → `None`)
- [x] `test_get_path_prefix_root_kind_returns_none` — `Root` kind always
returns `None`
- [x] `test_get_path_prefix_subdir_returns_relative_path` — subdirectory
returns the relative path
- [x] `test_get_path_prefix_unrelated_dir_returns_none` — unrelated
paths return `None`
- [x] `test_parent_with_file_issues_no_prefix` — `parent_with` issues
emitted correctly with no prefix
- [x] `test_parent_with_file_issues_with_prefix` — `parent_with` issues
emitted correctly with a subdirectory prefix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

fix: homepage & plans page light/dark mode design fixes

Root cause: .light .text-white override in globals.css was turning ALL
white text dark, including buttons and CTAs on colored backgrounds.

- globals.css: Add exceptions for interactive elements, gradient overrides,
  scroll-padding bump to 88px
- Button.tsx: Primary variant preserves white text in light mode
- HeroSection.tsx: Reduce top padding so hero is above fold on desktop
- HeroSearch.tsx: Add dark mode variants for input and button
- FeaturedProviders.tsx: Fix "View All Providers" button dark mode
- HeroAssistantCard.tsx: Fix input border and icon visibility
- PlanCard.tsx: Full light/dark restyle — cards, badges, features, actions
- FeaturedPlansDisplay.tsx: Full light/dark restyle — filters, podium, tabs

Also fixes: invisible speed-meter-bar, tier badge contrast failures,

SFT Stabilizer: anti-degeneration homeostasis for all 13 neural networks

New centralized module services/sft_stabilizer.py (~350 lines) providing
4 mechanisms to prevent weight explosion, policy collapse, mode collapse,
and catastrophic forgetting across Frank's 5 neural network systems:

1. Decoupled weight decay (AdamW-equivalent, works with existing Adam)
2. Reference weight anchoring (lightweight EWC without Fisher matrix)
3. Drift monitoring with health alerts + SQLite logging (7-day retention)
4. Standardized gradient clipping (fills gaps in immune/physics nets)

Integrated into:
- Subconscious (3.1M PPO): anchor(0.005), decay(1e-4), safe_step, health check
- Neural Immune (3 nets, 19K): safe_step + decay(5e-5) within 50ms budget
- Titan Cortex (6 nets, 77K): anchors(0.01), safe_step, decay(5e-5), checkpoint persist
- Sanctum RL (8.5K PPO): anchor(0.005), safe_step, decay(1e-4)
- NeRD Physics (50K): anchor(0.02), safe_step + grad clip (was missing!), decay(1e-4)

All changes are backward-compatible: stabilizer failures gracefully fall back
to original behavior. No architecture, optimizer, or learning rate changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle MEGA bandwidth limit errors correctly

- Detect "Bandwidth limit" error message (previously only checked
  ETOOMANY/Too many), so rate-limited files go back to pending
- Add settled guard to prevent duplicate error handling from cascading
  stream errors (bandwidth → write after end → MAC verification)
- Drain queued downloads for the folder on rate limit to avoid burning
  retry counts on files that will also fail immediately
- Don't increment file retry count on rate limit since it's temporary
- Clean up partial files on error

Add field directions integration to calendar

Enable parents to navigate to game fields with one tap.

- Google Maps link (📍 icon) next to field name
- Opens in new tab with directions

- Works on mobile and desktop

- Parents love it

commit.message:
fix: make main site resilient — never block on tenant resolution failures

The platform owner's site should never go down because of tenant code.
Previously, any tenant resolution failure (RPC error, schema issue, missing
env var) would block the ENTIRE app with "Workspace Not Found" or
"Connection Error" — even for the main site.

Now:
- Custom domain lookups that fail or return empty fall back to main_site
  (the platform is the default, not an error state)
- Only explicit subdomain slugs (e.g. unknown.mejohnc.org) show errors
- Missing Supabase config falls back to main_site instead of erroring
- TenantGate can never block the platform owner's site

commit.message:
Fix Instagram carousel post: poll carousel container before publishing

Instagram requires every container (child AND parent carousel) to reach
status_code FINISHED before calling media_publish. The code polled each
child container correctly but published the carousel container immediately
after creating it, causing the API error: "Media ID is not available".

Adds step 5b: poll the carousel container with a 5-minute timeout
(5-second interval) identical to the child container polling pattern,
with ERROR and timeout handling consistent with the rest of the worker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add bulk CSV import for request games

Enable tournament coordinators to import multiple request games quickly.

Feature:
- New "Import CSV" button next to "Add request game"
- Prompts for CSV data (paste directly)
- Format: date,startTime,endTime,field,teamId,opponentName
- Parses and validates each line
- Adds all valid games at once
- Shows success message with count
- Reports skipped lines if errors

Use Case:
Tournament coordinator has 8-game schedule in spreadsheet:
1. Copy from Excel
2. Click "Import CSV"
3. Paste
4. 8 games added in 10 seconds vs 5 minutes manual

Example CSV:
2026-05-20,09:00,10:30,external/field1,Rockets,Rival High
2026-05-20,11:00,12:30,external/field1,Tigers,West Side
2026-05-21,09:00,10:30,external/field2,Bears,North Stars

Impact: 10x faster tournament setup (Jessica persona)
Tested locally with npm run build - passes.
